fields.description:
# Attested signing: pin SHA, record verified_at_sha, in-toto-shaped HMAC, claim-gate coherence

## Why

A signed verdict is only worth anything if it is reproducible and bound to specific content.
Today the completion verdict signs a trivial manifest and stores `head_sha` as audit metadata
only — nothing pins WHAT code was verified. Attested mode must resolve->pin->record->sign the
immutable SHA, and the unsigned local mode must be structurally barred from signing.

## What

- In attested mode, completion-verification (and any signing gate) records `verified_at_sha`
  (the resolved immutable SHA) on the ticket and includes it in the signed payload.
- **`verified_at_sha` write is atomic + idempotent under retry** (set-once for a given SHA;
  a re-verification against a DIFFERENT SHA OVERWRITES the pin so no stale SHA persists), so
  concurrent or re-run signing cannot leave an inconsistent pin.
- Bar signing in `local` source mode: a local/dirty run verifies + reports but produces NO
  signature (the same deliberate no-signature signal `--force-close` leaves).
- Shape the signed bytes as an in-toto Statement (subject = `{sha}`, predicate = the verdict)
  so a future move to DSSE + asymmetric signatures + a transparency log (sigstore/cosign) is
  an envelope swap, not a data-model rewrite. KEEP HMAC for now (single trust domain: signer
  == verifier under the env key); its limits (no non-repudiation, no public verifiability, no
  transparency) are DOCUMENTED in S6.
- Coherence with the plan-review claim gate (ADR 0002): plan-review binds the `file_impact`
  `{path:hash}` map AT the pinned SHA; the claim gate's freshness re-check must hash those
  paths at the SAME ref basis. Enforce via a SINGLE shared ref-resolution boundary (not two
  independently-coded hash sites); update ADR 0002 to record this.

## Scope

- In: `verified_at_sha` record+sign (atomic/idempotent/overwrite-on-reverify), local-mode
  signing bar, in-toto Statement envelope, claim-gate freshness ref-coherence + ADR 0002 update.
- Out: the snapshot/cache (S1/S2); gate parameter threading (S3). DEPENDS ON S6 for the
  HMAC-trust-model docs (a dependency, not an acceptance criterion of this slice).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] An attested PASS records `verified_at_sha` on the ticket and signs over it; re-running
      against the same SHA reproduces a verdict over identical content.
- [ ] The `verified_at_sha` write is atomic + idempotent under retry; re-verifying against a
      different SHA overwrites the pin (no stale SHA persists, no double-pin).
- [ ] A `local`/dirty run never emits a signature (verify-and-report only).
- [ ] The signed payload is an in-toto Statement (subject digest + verdict predicate); the
      HMAC envelope is swappable without changing the statement shape.
- [ ] Plan-review's `file_impact` hashing and the claim-gate freshness re-check resolve the
      ref through ONE shared boundary (same pinned-SHA basis); a negative test asserts the two
      gates hash at the SAME ref (guarding against whole-HEAD divergence) rather than snapshotting output.
